banner: DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED — review with a human before proceeding
scenario: Illustrative mock applicant: Wei Ling Tan, a Singapore Citizen who lost her NRIC while commuting and is applying for a first-loss replacement online, with no change of particulars. All names, numbers, and addresses below are fabricated test data, not a real applicant's information.

Optional; this mock applicant has not registered a married name.
This scenario is a plain loss replacement with no accompanying change of particulars.

documentsProvided.value: S$100 first-loss fee, paid via PayNow (see paymentMethod).

documentsNotApplicable.reason: This mock applicant is reporting for herself, not via a medical-incapacity proxy; see VERIFICATION.md on this document's unconfirmed trigger condition.
reasonForNameChange is not applicable in this scenario, so no supporting document is required.

attestation: declarationInformationTrue, consentPhotographReuse, declarationNoForeignCitizenship, and acknowledgeNoRefundIfRecovered are all declared true by this mock applicant, dated declarationDate — see VERIFICATION.md for the exact source text of each.
safetyNotice: This packet was assembled by an automated agent from a GovSchema document (sg/ica/identity-card-replacement). GovSchema describes and verifies government processes; it does not submit them. No value in this packet has been transmitted to ICA or any government system, and the NRIC number, name, address, and all other personal details above are entirely fabricated test data, not a real applicant's information — this schema's own author confirmed (see VERIFICATION.md) that the primary self-service e-Service is Singpass-authenticated, so no live end-to-end test submission was attempted. A human (the applicant) must review every value below, confirm it is correct, and explicitly authorize submission through ICA's own channel (https://eservices.ica.gov.sg/esvclandingpage/iconline, requires Singpass login) before this application is filed. This packet is not a government record and carries no legal effect on its own.
